rulings.Case Title: BRANDILYNN S. THOMAS VS. JACQUELYNN SMITH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Re: Apn 7003-040, 274 Byxbee Street, San Francisco, Ca 94132
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 19, 2022 line 1. PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER RE: APN 7003-040, 274 BYXBEE STREET, SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94132 is DENIED without prejudice to be brought after probate procedure is completed for Theodore Thomas. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BOB BALDWIN VS. ANDREW PAUL BROUGHTON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Bob Baldwin' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, From Defendant Andrew Paul Broughton And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 19, 2022 line 2. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, FROM DEFENDANT ANDREW PAUL BROUGHTON AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS is GRANTED as follows: As to Nos. 3-10, 13-15, 26-30, and 35-38, Defendants are ordered to provide further responses without the overbroad objection. To the extent that there are privileged documents, a code-compliant privilege log shall be produced. As to Nos. 27-30, Defendants are ordered to provide further responses without the overbroad, confidentiality, and proprietary information objections and produce all non-privileged responsive documents. To the extent that there are privileged documents, a code-compliant privilege log shall be produced. As to Nos. 32-34, Defendants are ordered to remove any and all non-code compliant limitations verbiage. To the extent that there are privileged documents, a code-compliant privilege log shall be produced. Sanctions granted in the amount of $2,000 to be paid within 30 days of notice of entry of order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: THE TIDES CENTER, A CALIFORNIA NON-PROFIT PUBLIC VS. KANE EDWARDS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Notice Of Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 19, 2022 line 6. DEFENDANT KANE EDWARDS' MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT Hearing Required. No later than 9:00 a.m. on July 19, 2022 counsel to file (serve and courtesy copy delivered) a declaration attaching a proposed answer (code complaint with each asserted affirmative defense, if any, supported by factual allegations). The Court notes that while the courtesy copy of Declaration of Kelly Barnett delivered to the Court included 14 paragraphs and a proposed general denial, the filed declaration is 13 paragraphs long and has no attachments. The Court reminds counsel that the courtesy copies must be the true and correct copies of documents on file. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
